Search all .s and .S files in /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv (including rv32e/ subdirectory) for the following optimization opportunity pattern:

The pattern to find is: a `lw` instruction loading from offset 0 of a register, immediately followed by `addi reg, reg, 4` on that same register, where this occurs just before a loop. Inside the loop, the first load also uses offset 0 of the same register (before the addi inside the loop advances it).

The optimization is: remove the pre-loop `addi reg, reg, 4`, and change the first `lw` inside the loop from `0(reg)` to `4(reg)`. This saves one c.addi instruction (2 bytes when compiled with C extension).

Example from sf_cos.s (the already-optimized version for reference):
```
	lw	x14, 0(x18)             # load first coeff
#	addi	x18, x18, 4             # REMOVED - was here before optimization
.Lb13:
	mulhu	x14, x13, x14
	lw	x12, 4(x18)             # was 0(x18) before optimization
	addi	x18, x18, 4             # this one stays (loop advance)
	sub	x14, x12, x14
	bgez	x12, .Lb13
```

Please read the following files and identify which ones contain the unoptimized version of this pattern:
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/sf_sin.S
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/sf_tan.s

- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/sf_pow.s (if exists)
- Any other sf_*.s or s_*.s files
- rv32e/ equivalents of the above

Also check the andes_*.s polynomial evaluator files:
- /home/nick/work/picolibc_agentic/libm/machine/riscv/andes_p4.s
